title: Molten Nutty Korean Spaghetti Songpyeon
description: A lacto-vegetarian Korean dish featuring spaghetti, nuts, and a molten center, served in an agave leaf vessel, perfect for a sick day
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the spaghetti in boiling salted water until al dente, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and saute the chopped walnuts until fragrant.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
In a large bowl, combine the spaghetti mixture and grated mozzarella cheese, and mix until the cheese is well combined.
Spoon the spaghetti mixture onto the center of an agave leaf, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the agave leaf over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Place the songpyeon on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the agave leaf is crispy.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
